source: Current Beads graph after WPHX-013 baseline/bootstrap decisions

issues.title: WPHX-000 — Program governance and baseline
WPHX-000.07 — Reconcile remaining PRD browser and release worksets into Beads
issues.description: Seed the PRD-defined browser platform, Gutenberg package, classic browser/vendor, and validation/release worksets that are currently absent from the authoritative Beads graph. Preserve stable external references, parentage, dependencies, and acceptance criteria so parent completion reflects the PRD rather than only the initial bootstrap subset.
issues.acceptance_criteria: WPHX-400 has WPHX-401 through WPHX-409 children; WPHX-500 and WPHX-600 plus their PRD child worksets exist with PRD dependencies; WPHX-700 has WPHX-701 through WPHX-714 children; deterministic seed validation, graph validation, export, receipts, and progress-matrix documentation pass.

issues.title: WPHX-001 — Initialize repository, licenses, contribution rules, generated-file policy
issues.acceptance_criteria: Clean bootstrap and agent instructions are committed and pushed.

issues.title: WPHX-002 — Lock Haxe 4.3.7, genes-ts, PHP backend source, Node/npm, PHP images
issues.acceptance_criteria: toolchain.lock.json exists and a clean build receipt is recorded.

issues.title: WPHX-003 — Obtain full WordPress 7.0.0 checkout and official distribution
issues.acceptance_criteria: WordPress commit/tag, ZIP checksum, and test presence are recorded.

issues.title: WPHX-004 — Resolve and lock WordPress 7.0 Gutenberg commit/artifact
issues.acceptance_criteria: Core Gutenberg pin is verified against WordPress metadata/build.

issues.title: WPHX-005 — Lock attached Gutenberg 23.4.0 forward ref
issues.acceptance_criteria: Git commit matches the package version and snapshot baseline.

issues.title: WPHX-006 — Generate complete source/artifact/test inventory
issues.acceptance_criteria: No executable source or shipped executable artifact is unclassified.

issues.title: WPHX-007 — Define manifest schemas and validators
issues.acceptance_criteria: Schema tests pass and output is deterministic.

issues.title: WPHX-008 — Build vanilla oracle environments
issues.acceptance_criteria: PHP, DB, and browser baselines produce receipts.

issues.title: WPHX-009 — Create initial parity dashboard
issues.acceptance_criteria: Dashboard can query by file, API, package, task, and gate.

issues.title: WPHX-010 — Seed Beads epics, issues, external refs, and dependencies
issues.acceptance_criteria: bd ready --json yields only valid ready work.

issues.title: WPHX-011 — License/provenance audit
issues.acceptance_criteria: Licenses, notices, and vendor origins are recorded.

issues.title: WPHX-012 — Decide release and forward baseline branch policy
issues.acceptance_criteria: ADR accepted and baseline mixing is prevented.

issues.title: WPHX-013 — Decide and bootstrap GutenbergHX sibling repository protocol
issues.description: Record when and how to create a sibling gutenberghx repo that cross-references wordpress-hx without fragmenting task authority. Include smaller dependent-library policy and cross-repo manifest/Beads conventions.
issues.acceptance_criteria: ADR or operations doc defines trigger criteria, repo relationship, Beads authority, cross-repo references, and small-library split policy before any gutenberghx bootstrap.

issues.title: WPHX-014 — Define HHX/template authoring strategy for WordPress templates
issues.description: Use haxe.elixir.codex, haxe.ruby/RailsHx, and tink_hxx references to decide when WordPress PHP/HTML templates, block markup, admin screens, and Gutenberg TSX-like surfaces should use HHX-style typed inline markup versus external/original template shells.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Documented policy names source references, allowed HHX use cases, escape hatches, fixture gates, and cases that must stay original-path PHP/template shells until linker/template feasibility is proven.

issues.title: WPHX-015 — Define wrapper-to-Haxe ownership state model
issues.description: Define manifest states for external oracle, temporary bridge/wrapper, scaffolded, Haxe-owned parity candidate, and verified Haxe-owned implementation. Include removal gates so typed compatibility layers do not become the destination.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Manifest schema/docs encode ownership states, temporary wrappers require removal conditions, and smell-fix/refactor policy is reflected in task templates.

issues.title: WPHX-016 — Evaluate Beads CLI upgrade from 0.49.0
issues.description: bd doctor reports 1.0.4 available while this repo is pinned to 0.49.0. Evaluate upgrade impact on hooks, JSONL sync, merge driver, and AGENTS workflow before changing the pin.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Decision recorded; if upgraded, .beads/.local_version, toolchain.lock.json, docs/operations/beads.md, hooks, and a clean bd doctor receipt are updated together.

issues.title: WPHX-100 — PHP target feasibility
WPHX-101 — Stock Haxe PHP smoke project and generated-output snapshot
issues.description: Create a reusable stock Haxe PHP smoke fixture, compile it with the locked Haxe toolchain, run the generated PHP on the local PHP runtime, and record a generated-output snapshot for later PHP target regressions.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Haxe 4.3.7 compiles and runs the smoke fixture on the PHP matrix, and the generated output snapshot is recorded with a receipt.

issues.title: WPHX-102 — F1 global function facade fixture
issues.description: Build the first global-function facade fixture for WordPress-style conditional API availability.
issues.acceptance_criteria: The fixture proves exact ABI and conditional availability or records a minimized blocker.

issues.title: WPHX-103 — F2 reference fixture suite
issues.description: Exercise PHP reference boundaries required by WordPress APIs and hooks.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Required reference cases pass or a minimized compiler/backend blocker is recorded.

issues.title: WPHX-104 — F3 native values/global fixture suite
issues.description: Exercise PHP native values, arrays, globals, and superglobal-shaped boundaries.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Native semantics match the oracle or a minimized blocker is recorded.

issues.title: WPHX-105 — F4 public class fixture suite
issues.description: Exercise public class/interface contracts that WordPress plugins can reflect or instantiate.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Class contracts pass or backend customization need is proven.

issues.title: WPHX-106 — F5 include/load fixture suite
issues.description: Validate ordered original-path shell loading and include-time side effects.
issues.acceptance_criteria: The ordered shell model is validated or blocked with minimized evidence.

issues.title: WPHX-107 — F6 template/caller-scope fixture suite
issues.description: Validate WordPress-style PHP/HTML template shells and caller-scope variable behavior.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Two real-style template fixtures pass or a minimized linker/template blocker is recorded.

issues.title: WPHX-108 — F7 hook kernel fixture
issues.description: Validate the first WordPress hook kernel fixture against upstream hook traces.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Hook traces match the oracle or a minimized blocker is recorded.

issues.title: WPHX-109 — ADR: macros/linker/backend boundary
issues.description: Use F1-F7 evidence to decide what belongs in ordinary Haxe, macros, the deterministic linker, or a minimal PHP backend fork.
issues.acceptance_criteria: An ADR records the accepted architecture and whether a backend fork is required.

issues.title: WPHX-200 — PHP ABI, runtime, and linker
issues.description: Turn the PHP feasibility evidence into the typed ABI, native boundary, generated facade, original-path linker, and conformance machinery needed before WordPress core source-unit porting.
issues.acceptance_criteria: WPHX-201 through WPHX-208 and WPHX-210 are complete, with WPHX-209 opened only if a future ADR/compiler blocker requires a PHP backend fork.

issues.title: WPHX-201 — PHP ABI extractor from upstream AST
issues.description: Extract a deterministic PHP ABI manifest from the locked WordPress upstream source using structured parsing rather than ad hoc text matching.
issues.acceptance_criteria: A full Core ABI manifest with stable hashes is generated and validated.

issues.title: WPHX-202 — @:wp.* macro contract and compile-time validator
issues.description: Define the typed Haxe annotations and macro validation contract that binds Haxe declarations to WordPress ABI manifest entries.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Invalid bindings fail compilation with deterministic diagnostics.

issues.title: WPHX-203 — Native WordPress PHP boundary types
issues.description: Codify native PHP boundary types for WordPress arrays, globals, callables, references, and WP_Error-style values.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Conformance tests cover arrays, globals, callables, references, and errors.

issues.title: WPHX-204 — Deterministic global facade generator
issues.description: Generate original-name PHP global function facades from the ABI manifest and typed Haxe bindings.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Generated wrappers reproduce the ABI manifest deterministically.

issues.title: WPHX-205 — Original-path shell and file-segment linker
issues.description: Build the deterministic file linker that emits original-path PHP shells and maps generated Haxe segments into the WordPress distribution layout.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Load and template fixtures pass from the generated distribution layout.

issues.title: WPHX-206 — Public class/interface/trait emission
issues.description: Emit public WordPress class, interface, and trait shells with native names and reflection-compatible contracts.
issues.acceptance_criteria: The exact public class/interface/trait fixture suite is green.

issues.title: WPHX-207 — Debug/source-map/error-path support
issues.description: Preserve useful traces across generated PHP, Haxe source, and original WordPress source units.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Traces map to Haxe and original source units.

issues.title: WPHX-208 — PHP compiler conformance CI
issues.description: Turn the PHP target fixtures and ABI/linker conformance checks into a required CI matrix for compiler/runtime-sensitive changes.
issues.acceptance_criteria: The matrix runs on every target/runtime change.

issues.title: WPHX-210 — Build profiles: debug, parity, release
issues.description: Define deterministic build profiles for developer debugging, parity validation, and release artifact generation.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Profile outputs are deterministic and documented, with intentional differences recorded.

issues.title: WPHX-400 — genes-ts/browser platform
WPHX-401 — Pin and build genes-ts in both output modes
issues.description: Verify the locked genes-ts checkout and build it in both supported output modes before package translation begins.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Both genes-ts output modes have deterministic CI snapshots and runtime smoke evidence.

issues.title: WPHX-402 — Generate JS/TS package, module, and API inventory
issues.description: Generate the browser-side package, module, export, artifact, and API inventory for the locked WordPress and Gutenberg baselines.
issues.acceptance_criteria: All relevant workspaces, modules, exports, and shipped browser artifacts are classified.

issues.title: WPHX-403 — F8 ts2hx leaf-package spike
issues.description: Run the F8 feasibility spike on a selected real leaf package using the locked genes-ts compiler.
issues.acceptance_criteria: The selected leaf package builds with strict types and passes differential runtime tests, or minimized generic compiler blockers are recorded.

issues.title: WPHX-404 — F9 React/TSX spike
issues.description: Run the F9 React/TSX feasibility spike over a representative WordPress package slice.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Browser, visual, accessibility, and source-map parity pass, or minimized generic compiler blockers are recorded.

issues.title: WPHX-405 — WordPress package, export, and script-handle profile
issues.description: Define the compiler/linker profile that preserves WordPress package exports, script handles, globals, and Core asset metadata.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Generated package and WordPress Core asset metadata match the locked oracle contracts.

issues.title: WPHX-406 — JS-native boundary types and extern policy
issues.description: Define typed browser-native boundaries and extern policy without normalizing broad Dynamic or generated any use.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Null, undefined, object, callable, and async boundary fixtures pass with documented narrow escape hatches.

issues.title: WPHX-407 — Browser differential harness
issues.description: Build deterministic Node and browser oracle/candidate comparisons for generated browser packages.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Node and Playwright comparisons emit attributable deterministic parity reports.

issues.title: WPHX-408 — Bundle, source-map, and determinism pipeline
issues.description: Produce reproducible development and minified browser outputs with source maps and WordPress-compatible package topology.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Development and minified bundles plus source maps reproduce byte-for-byte under the locked toolchain.

issues.title: WPHX-409 — genes-ts compiler-pressure CI
issues.description: Gate genes-ts changes with minimized generic fixtures discovered by the WordPress and Gutenberg browser spikes.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Minimized fixtures and compiler receipts gate every relied-on genes-ts change.

issues.title: WPHX-500 — Gutenberg packages
issues.description: Port the locked WordPress distribution and forward Gutenberg package waves after browser feasibility passes.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Applicable Gutenberg package, API, runtime, integration, and artifact manifests reach their PRD ownership gates.

issues.title: WPHX-501 — Wave J1 pure leaf packages
issues.description: Port the first wave of pure Gutenberg leaf packages selected by the package inventory.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Selected J1 packages have package API and runtime parity records.

issues.title: WPHX-502 — Wave J2 foundational packages
issues.description: Port foundational Gutenberg packages after the pure leaf wave is proven.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Selected J2 packages have package API and runtime parity records.

issues.title: WPHX-503 — Wave J3 data, composition, and router packages
issues.description: Port the data, composition, and router package wave.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Selected J3 packages pass package and integration parity gates.

issues.title: WPHX-504 — Wave J4 UI, components, and blocks foundations
issues.description: Port the Gutenberg UI, component, and block foundation wave.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Selected J4 packages pass package, React, visual, and accessibility parity gates.

issues.title: WPHX-505 — Wave J5 core-data, interface, and global services
issues.description: Port core-data, interface, and global service packages.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Selected J5 packages pass data, API, and editor integration parity gates.

issues.title: WPHX-506 — @wordpress/block-editor
issues.description: Port the @wordpress/block-editor package against the locked distribution baseline.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Package and editor integration parity gates pass.

issues.title: WPHX-507 — @wordpress/block-library JS and PHP joint workset
issues.description: Port the block-library browser surface while preserving its joint PHP registration and rendering contracts.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Block registration, edit, save, and render parity gates pass.

issues.title: WPHX-508 — @wordpress/editor and global styles
issues.description: Port @wordpress/editor and the browser-side global styles integration.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Editor data and UI parity gates pass.

issues.title: WPHX-509 — edit-post, edit-site, widgets, customize, and fields
issues.description: Port the major Gutenberg application roots after the editor package wave.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Application end-to-end, visual, and accessibility gates pass.

issues.title: WPHX-510 — Boot, build, initialization, routes, and integration roots
issues.description: Port Gutenberg boot, build, initialization, routing, and integration roots needed by the WordPress distribution.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Core integration artifacts match the locked WordPress distribution contracts.

issues.title: WPHX-511 — Gutenberg build, test, and development packages
issues.description: Close the Gutenberg build, test, and development package workspaces after runtime package waves establish the required compiler surface.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Applicable non-runtime workspaces satisfy the C4 workspace closure contract.

issues.title: WPHX-512 — Forward Gutenberg 23.4 manifest closure
issues.description: Reconcile every locked forward Gutenberg 23.4 package record with its ownership and parity state.
issues.acceptance_criteria: All 123 forward Gutenberg package records are resolved.

issues.title: WPHX-600 — WordPress classic JS and browser vendors
issues.description: Port first-party classic WordPress browser code and govern bundled browser-vendor replacement after browser feasibility passes.
issues.acceptance_criteria: First-party browser and bundled browser-vendor manifests reach their PRD ownership gates.

issues.title: WPHX-601 — Inventory classic WordPress enqueued scripts and globals
issues.description: Inventory classic WordPress browser scripts, handles, globals, dependencies, and build outputs.
issues.acceptance_criteria: The classic script handle, global, and build graph is complete.

issues.title: WPHX-602 — Common and admin classic scripts
issues.description: Port common and admin classic WordPress scripts against their PHP request and screen contracts.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Selected common and admin request and end-to-end parity gates pass.

issues.title: WPHX-603 — Media JavaScript
issues.description: Port the WordPress media browser runtime and preserve its PHP/media integration behavior.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Media end-to-end parity gates pass.

issues.title: WPHX-604 — Customizer, widgets, and legacy editor scripts
issues.description: Port Customizer, widget, and legacy editor browser scripts with their PHP integration surfaces.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Customizer, widget, and legacy editor end-to-end parity gates pass.

issues.title: WPHX-605 — Dependency extraction and Core asset copy integration
issues.description: Integrate generated browser outputs into WordPress dependency extraction and official distribution asset paths.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Official asset paths, dependency metadata, and script handles match the WordPress distribution.

issues.title: WPHX-606 — Bundled browser-vendor provenance and source acquisition
issues.description: Resolve source and provenance for browser vendors shipped by the vanilla WordPress distribution.
issues.acceptance_criteria: No bundled browser vendor has an unknown or minified-only origin.

issues.title: WPHX-607 — Bundled browser-vendor Haxe ports
issues.description: Port bundled browser vendors only where the provenance and replacement gates choose direct Haxe ownership.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Selected browser-vendor ports pass vendor tests and consumer parity gates.

issues.title: WPHX-608 — First-party browser manifest closure
issues.description: Reconcile the WordPress distribution's first-party browser records after Gutenberg and classic browser worksets close.
issues.acceptance_criteria: The C2 first-party browser ownership tier is verified.

issues.title: WPHX-609 — Browser-vendor manifest closure
issues.description: Reconcile every bundled browser-vendor record after vendor replacement and exception gates close.
issues.acceptance_criteria: The C3 browser-vendor ownership portion is verified.

issues.title: WPHX-700 — Parity, security, performance, and distribution
WPHX-701 — Upstream PHP test integration
issues.description: Expand selected upstream PHP ratchets into an attributable normalized comparison lane for generated WordPress PHP outputs.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Upstream PHP results have deterministic attribution and normalized oracle/candidate comparison.

issues.title: WPHX-702 — Upstream Gutenberg test integration
issues.description: Run applicable upstream Gutenberg package and end-to-end suites against generated Haxe-owned browser outputs.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Applicable package and end-to-end suites execute against Haxe outputs with attributable results.

issues.title: WPHX-703 — PHP ABI and file-topology comparator
issues.description: Compare the generated PHP distribution's public ABI and original-path file topology with the WordPress 7.0 oracle.
issues.acceptance_criteria: There are zero unexplained critical PHP ABI or file-topology deltas.

issues.title: WPHX-704 — JS package, API, and artifact comparator
issues.description: Compare generated browser packages, APIs, exports, and shipped artifacts with the locked WordPress and Gutenberg oracles.
issues.acceptance_criteria: There are zero unexplained critical JS package, API, or artifact deltas.

issues.title: WPHX-705 — Differential HTTP, DB, and hook trace runner
issues.description: Generalize bounded PHP parity runners into deterministic installed HTTP, database, and hook trace comparisons.
issues.acceptance_criteria: A deterministic trace schema and oracle/candidate comparisons cover the admitted critical slices.

issues.title: WPHX-706 — Plugin compatibility fixture suite
issues.description: Exercise representative plugins against generated WordPress distributions without hidden upstream core fallback.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Fixture plugins pass install, activation, runtime, deactivation, and ABI compatibility gates.

issues.title: WPHX-707 — Theme compatibility fixture suite
issues.description: Exercise representative classic and block themes against generated WordPress distributions.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Classic and block theme compatibility and rendering parity gates pass.

issues.title: WPHX-708 — Curated ecosystem corpus
issues.description: Define and execute a reproducible ecosystem corpus after the core installed trace and fixture suites establish the harness.
issues.acceptance_criteria: The ecosystem corpus is declared, reproducible, and emits attributable compatibility results.

issues.title: WPHX-709 — Visual and accessibility regression suite
issues.description: Build visual and accessibility regression coverage for critical admin, editor, and front-end flows.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Critical flows remain within accepted visual and accessibility baselines.

issues.title: WPHX-710 — Security equivalence review and fuzz/property suites
issues.description: Review the complete generated distribution security boundary and run fuzz/property suites over parity-sensitive inputs.
issues.acceptance_criteria: No unresolved critical or high security issues remain at the replacement gate.

issues.title: WPHX-711 — Performance and memory benchmark suite
issues.description: Measure generated distribution performance and memory from the walking skeleton through release candidates.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Budgets are measured and every material regression is attributed.

issues.title: WPHX-712 — Reproducible distribution builder and SBOM
issues.description: Build the complete WordPress-compatible distribution reproducibly with provenance, notices, and an SBOM.
issues.acceptance_criteria: The generated distribution is checksummed and reproducible, with complete provenance, notices, and SBOM output.

issues.title: WPHX-713 — Experimental update and rollback channel
issues.description: Prove an experimental update channel that cannot be silently overwritten by vanilla Core and has a tested rollback path.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Vanilla overwrite is prevented and the rollback drill passes.

issues.title: WPHX-714 — Release dossier and parity scorecard
issues.description: Assemble the release dossier and parity scorecard only after all required technical and operational gate tasks close.
issues.acceptance_criteria: The dossier links reproducible evidence for every compatibility contract and PRD gate.

issues.title: WPHX-800 — Codex + Beads program operations
WPHX-801 — Pin Beads version and initialize the root database
issues.acceptance_criteria: bd --version is locked; root .beads works; no nested active store.

issues.title: WPHX-802 — Install/review Codex integration and author AGENTS hierarchy
issues.acceptance_criteria: Codex reports expected instruction sources and root guidance remains concise.

issues.title: WPHX-803 — Implement deterministic PRD-key to Beads-issue seeding
issues.acceptance_criteria: Stable WPHX external refs import/update idempotently.

issues.title: WPHX-804 — Generate bounded task packets from Beads and manifests
issues.acceptance_criteria: One command emits reproducible context for a selected issue.

issues.title: WPHX-805 — Link verification receipts and manifest digests to Beads issues
issues.acceptance_criteria: Close validation rejects missing or stale evidence.

issues.title: WPHX-806 — Prove multi-agent claim/worktree/handoff protocol
issues.acceptance_criteria: Two-agent drill has no duplicate claims, path collisions, or lost discoveries.

issues.title: WPHX-807 — Configure Dolt sync plus bd backup and perform restore drill
issues.acceptance_criteria: Fresh clone/restore reproduces tasks, dependencies, memory, and ready queue.
